topic: Exec/shell/code-interpreter tool design across major AI agent harnesses and libraries
notes: This guide is a design-space deep-dive, complementing harness-tool-surface-audit.md which covers the ship-list. Each source is scored on Authority (official vs blog), Recency (2025-2026 content prioritized), Depth (design rationale vs feature checklist), Examples (schemas, code), and Uniqueness (does it add a dimension not covered elsewhere). Overall qualityScore = 3*authority + 2*recency + 2*depth + 2*examples + 1*uniqueness (max 100).

sources.title: Claude Code tools reference
keyInsights: Bash tool runs each command in a separate process (no persistent shell session), but cwd carries over across calls when inside project dir / additionalDirectories.
Environment variables do NOT persist across Bash calls; export FOO=bar in call 1 is invisible in call 2. CLAUDE_ENV_FILE is the escape hatch.
CLAUDE_BASH_MAINTAIN_PROJECT_WORKING_DIR=1 disables cwd carry-over entirely; subagent sessions never carry over.
If cd escapes project/additional dirs, Claude Code resets and appends 'Shell cwd was reset to <dir>' to tool result.
Monitor tool (v2.1.98+): background command → each output line fed back to model as event. Uses same permission rules as Bash.
PowerShell tool: preview on non-Windows; auto mode unsupported in preview; on Windows, sandboxing is not supported.
Monitor not available on Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry, or when telemetry is disabled.

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK overview
keyInsights: Agent SDK exposes Bash, Monitor, Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, WebFetch, WebSearch, AskUserQuestion as default tools.
Permissions: allowed_tools / allowedTools whitelist is the primary pre-approval knob; subagents inherit via AgentDefinition.tools.
Hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, SessionStart, SessionEnd, UserPromptSubmit) are the library-style permission pattern.
`permission_mode: 'acceptEdits'` pre-approves Edit/Write for autonomous flows.
Agent SDK is a superset of Client SDK — includes the tool loop + tool execution, not just message API.

sources.title: Codex CLI agent approvals & security
keyInsights: Three sandbox modes: read-only, workspace-write (default), danger-full-access.
Four approval policies: untrusted, on-request (default with --full-auto), never, granular.
macOS uses sandbox-exec Seatbelt; Linux uses bwrap + seccomp; Windows WSL2 uses Linux sandbox.
Protected paths (.git, .agents, .codex) stay read-only even under workspace-write.
Network disabled by default across all modes; enable via network_access=true in [sandbox_workspace_write] or per-permission network rules.
--yolo / --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox for full access.
Telemetry off by default; log_user_prompt=false recommended to avoid storing sensitive data.
Destructive tool calls always require approval when tool advertises destructive annotation.

sources.title: Codex CLI config reference
keyInsights: sandbox_mode values: read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access.
approval_policy: untrusted, on-request, never, granular (+legacy on-failure).
workspace-write sub-options: writable_roots, exclude_slash_tmp, exclude_tmpdir_env_var, network_access.
shell_environment_policy: inherit (all/core/none), set, include_only, exclude, ignore_default_excludes (preserves KEY/SECRET/TOKEN).
permissions.<name>.network: enabled, mode (limited/full), domains allowlist/denylist, unix_sockets, enable_socks5.
features.unified_exec is PTY-backed and defaults to enabled on non-Windows.
allow_login_shell defaults to true — .bashrc/.zshrc run and shape environment.

sources.title: OpenCode bash.ts source
keyInsights: Zod schema: command, timeout (ms, optional), workdir (optional), description (5-10 word summary, required).
Default timeout 2*60*1000 ms (2 min), overridable via OPENCODE_EXPERIMENTAL_BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS.
On timeout: process killed with 3-second force-kill window; metadata flag 'bash tool terminated command after exceeding timeout'.
Output capped at Truncate.MAX_LINES and Truncate.MAX_BYTES; overflow streamed to temp file; metadata points model at path.
Two permission types: external_directory (paths outside project) and bash (with source-pattern and arity-based prefixes).
Description text dynamically generated from bash.txt with ${directory}, ${os}, ${shell}, ${chaining}, ${maxLines}, ${maxBytes} substitutions.
workdir resolved via resolvePath() including Windows path normalization and POSIX→Windows via cygpath.

sources.title: Gemini CLI shell.ts source
keyInsights: ShellToolParams: command (req), description (opt), dir_path (opt), is_background (opt), delay_ms (opt, default 200ms), additional_permissions (opt).
Timeout is inactivity-based — getShellToolInactivityTimeout() resets on stdout/stderr data, binary detection, or exit.
OUTPUT_UPDATE_INTERVAL_MS = 1000 ms — batches updates to at most 1/second.
Binary detection halts stream immediately: '[Binary output detected. Halting stream...]'.
Network policy three-step: explicit additional_permissions.network → getProactiveToolSuggestions() inference → isNetworkReliantCommand() → auto-expand.
No hardcoded allowlist; command-prefix matching against stored approvals; session vs persistent approval modes.
Sandbox-denial parsing: parses denial message, extracts blocked path, re-requests with expanded permission set (self-healing loop).

sources.title: Gemini CLI shellBackgroundTools.ts source
keyInsights: Two tools: list_background_processes (no params) and read_background_output (pid req, lines opt default 100, delay_ms opt).
Processes tracked via PID in session-scoped registry; log files at ShellExecutionService.getLogFilePath(pid).
Session isolation: verifies process belongs to current session to prevent cross-session log reads.
Security: symlink detection, 64KB buffer cap.
Returns formatted list with PID, status, command, exit code, signal.

sources.title: Continue.dev runTerminalCommand definition
keyInsights: Minimal schema: command (string, req), waitForCompletion (bool, opt; true=foreground default).
Platform-specific shell: PowerShell for Windows, zsh/bash for Unix.
Explicitly documented as non-stateful across commands.
Background commands should be stopped via shell commands, not Ctrl+C.
File editing banned: description routes model to Edit/MultiEdit tools.
Commands requiring special privileges restricted.
Default permission: allowedWithPermission; security evaluated via evaluateTerminalCommandSecurity.

sources.title: OpenHands CmdRunAction & IPythonRunCellAction
keyInsights: CmdRunAction fields: command, is_input (feeds stdin to running PID — structural answer to interactive commands), thought, blocking, is_static, cwd, hidden, confirmation_state, security_risk.
Empty command prints current tmux window — poll-state affordance.
IPythonRunCellAction fields: code, thought, include_extra, kernel_init_code, confirmation_state, security_risk.
IPython action backed by Jupyter kernel — variables, imports, module state persist across calls.
kernel_init_code runs on kernel restart — recovery affordance.
Two separate actions because tmux shell state ≠ Jupyter kernel state; kernel is not a subprocess of shell.
Default is_static=false → runs in persistent tmux session; is_static=true → fresh subprocess.

sources.title: CodeAct: Executable Code Actions Elicit Better LLM Agents (Wang et al., ICLR 2025)
keyInsights: Unified Python action space outperforms predefined JSON tool schemas by up to 20% success rate on 17 LLMs tested.
Rationale: dynamic composition of tools, multi-turn adaptation, loop/conditionals, state reuse.
Motivates OpenHands' IPythonRunCellAction choice — Python-as-action is only maximally useful when kernel state persists.
Key advantage: model can execute code, observe, revise priors, emit new actions — iterative self-debugging.

sources.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces (Yang et al.)
keyInsights: Custom ACI significantly improves agent ability to navigate repos and execute tests beyond raw-bash baseline.
Wrapped commands filter noise for agent comprehension — raw shell output overwhelms models.
Achieved pass@1 of 12.5% on SWE-bench and 87.7% on HumanEvalFix with ACI design.
Motivates our recommendation to keep Bash as escape-hatch and invest in typed tools (Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob).

sources.title: SWE-agent ACI design rationale
keyInsights: Search commands list filenames only — 'extensive context proved too confusing for the model'.
File viewer shows exactly 100 lines/turn (deliberate constraint), with goto/scroll_up/scroll_down.
Edit commands gated by a linter — syntactically incorrect edits rejected before applying.
Empty-output convention: 'Your command ran successfully and did not produce any output.' — explicit acknowledgment.
Raw bash provides unfiltered output that overwhelms LMs; ACI wrappers optimize for agent decision-making.

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK tools
keyInsights: Four execution-related tools: CodeInterpreterTool (hosted OpenAI Python sandbox), ShellTool (hosted container or local executor), LocalShellTool (legacy local), ComputerTool (GUI/browser via Computer protocol).
ShellTool hosted modes: container_auto (per-request provisioning) or container_reference (reuse); network policy disabled/allowlist.
ShellTool local mode: async executor function, runs in your process (no sandbox by default).
CodeInterpreterTool runs in OpenAI-managed sandboxed environment; minimal schema.
ComputerTool requires local Computer/AsyncComputer implementation; emits screenshot/action sequences via Responses API.

sources.title: Cline executeCommandTool (TypeScript)
keyInsights: Schema: command (string), requires_approval (bool — model-supplied classifier of safety).
CommandExecutor delegates to CommandOrchestrator for output streaming + timeout.
Long-running commands use 'proceed while running' pattern: background tracking via trackBackgroundCommand; foreground tracking via currentProcess.
suppressUserInteraction option coordinates with updateBackgroundCommandState to prevent ask() dialog conflicts.
Interactive input supported via orchestrator.
Model-supplied requires_approval is a trust hole for autonomous agents — planning model should not be the permission source of truth.

sources.title: Cline terminal integration architecture
keyInsights: Architecture: CommandExecutor + CommandOrchestrator + constants + types + standalone mode.
Shell selection delegated to manager.getOrCreateTerminal() — platform-specific, not in executor.
Foreground + background dual-tracking pattern for long-running commands.

sources.title: MCP reference servers
keyInsights: Seven active reference servers: Everything, Fetch, Filesystem, Git, Memory, Sequential Thinking, Time.
NO canonical exec/shell/code-interpreter reference server exists.
Archived Puppeteer server offered browser-based JS execution; no shell server either archived or current.
Implication: MCP does not solve exec for tool libraries — tool must be first-class in the harness.

sources.title: AutoGen LocalCommandLineCodeExecutor
keyInsights: Constructor: timeout (default 60s), work_dir, functions, functions_module, cleanup_temp_files, virtual_env_context.
Supported languages: bash, shell, sh, pwsh, powershell, ps1, python — language as config, not tool parameter.
Each code block written to a file and executed as a separate subprocess (file-per-block).
Security: regex filter against dangerous commands.
Lifecycle: start(), stop(), restart().
create_default_code_executor() prefers DockerCommandLineCodeExecutor if Docker is available, falls back to local with warning.

sources.title: E2B Code Interpreter quickstart
keyInsights: Firecracker microVM per sandbox.
Python + JavaScript SDKs.
SDK methods: sandbox.commands.run() for shell; sandbox.runCode() / sandbox.run_code() for code.
Sandbox.create() lifecycle — persistent for sandbox lifetime; cleanup on delete.
Implies: a tool library should be sandbox-agnostic; E2B is a consumer of the Bash tool, not a competitor.

sources.title: Daytona sandbox for AI agents
keyInsights: Open-source, secure, elastic infrastructure for running AI-generated code.
SDKs in Python, TypeScript, Ruby, Go.
Sandboxes are 'full composable computers' with filesystem, Git, LSP, and pseudo-terminal.
Methods: sandbox.process.code_run() (code), sandbox.process.execute_command() (shell).
Lifecycle: create → use → delete.

sources.title: Armin Ronacher — Agentic Coding Tools
keyInsights: 'LLM chaos monkey' — tools must be bulletproof against incorrect usage patterns.
Tools need speed and clarity; 'crashes are tolerable, hangs are problematic'.
User-side sandbox answer: dev env in Docker; author disables all permission checks ('claude-yolo') and manages risk at container level.
Effective tools: simple, idempotent scripts + observability (log to files for agent self-diagnosis).
Avoid unnecessary output verbosity — token efficiency in agentic loops.

sources.title: Simon Willison — Claude Code coverage
keyInsights: Skeptical of AI-based safeguards: 'unconvinced by prompt injection protections that rely on AI, since they're non-deterministic by nature'.
Prefers robust sandbox by default: 'restricts file access and network connections in a deterministic way'.
Claude Code revenue / usage scale validates design pragmatism — permission system is good enough in practice.
Session log recovery noted as unexpected benefit — suggests audit/log-first design pays off.

sources.title: Firejail SUID sandbox
keyInsights: Linux namespaces + seccomp-bpf primitives; lightweight C with minimal dependencies.
Pre-configured profiles for 1000+ desktop apps.
Agent harness integration: prefix subprocess with `firejail`, use `--private`, `--net=none`, `--noprofile` for isolation.
Candidate for an @agent-sh/harness-sandbox-firejail adapter package.
